artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
To: Dr. Melissa Grant <mgrant@rivercountyhealth.mo.gov>
CC: Mark Reynolds <mreynolds@riverbendhs.org>, Emily Reynolds <emily.reynolds@student.riverbendhs.org>
Date: February 12, 2026
Subject: Endorsement letter & background details for Vision & Wellness initiative

Hi Melissa,

Thank you again for agreeing to write the endorsement for our Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative. The grant portal is asking me to confirm the endorsement date as 2026-02-20, which matches what you suggested for your clinic admin time next week, so I’ll plug that in unless you need me to adjust.

You asked for a few specifics that show how the project is uniquely youth‑driven. One key story is Emily’s experience in September 2022, when she failed her vision screening at Riverbend High School. When we took her to Dr. Carter’s office, we learned that her headaches and slipping grades in Algebra II were tied to significant astigmatism. That scare is what pushed me to insist that every Mobile Vision & Wellness Day include a pathway for students to get same‑week optometry appointments, which we piloted with Riverbend Vision Center in November 2022.

I also think the endorsement should be candid about our learning curve. In the first year we tried this, turnout was pretty low and we even had to cancel one of the planned screening days because only a handful of people signed up in advance. That experience is what led us to partner more intentionally with the Leo Club and the school counselors.

If it helps, I can drop by your office on the 19th with our latest data summary and a one‑page timeline. Otherwise, feel free to email me a draft and I’ll just confirm receipt for the application file.

With appreciation,
Patricia

Patricia Marie Reynolds
Community Health Program Coordinator
Riverbend Community Health Partnership
Service Chairperson, Riverbend Lions Club

metadata.subject: Endorsement letter & background details for Vision & Wellness initiative

artifacts.content:
From: grants@riverbendhospitalfoundation.org
To: patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org
Subject: Clarification on community partner endorsement timing
Date: 2026-01-29

Dear Ms. Reynolds,

Thank you for submitting the updated Vision & Wellness Outreach proposal and for attaching the draft endorsement letter from the Riverbend Community Health Partnership.

Before our board packet goes out, we need to confirm the timing of your executive director’s formal endorsement. The draft you sent references “our forthcoming endorsement in mid-March 2026,” but the cover email from your director mentioned that she hoped to sign no later than February 10, 2026 so it could be included with your LCIF-style budget narrative.

Because our trustees sometimes ask about alignment between grant decisions and partner endorsements, it would help to know the exact date you expect the endorsement letter to be signed and placed on letterhead. If it ends up being after our March 1 review meeting, we can still proceed, but the award would be contingent on receiving the signed endorsement by April 15, 2026.

I also appreciated your candid explanation in the narrative about your own hospitalization at Riverbend General Hospital in March 2023 after nearly fainting during a Saturday clinic at the Riverbend Food Share. Your note about Dr. Lawson adjusting your medications and urging you to scale back from 50–60 volunteer hours a month to closer to 25–30 helped our committee understand your delegation plan.

Please reply with the anticipated endorsement signature date so I can note it in our internal summary.

Best regards,

Linda Chavez
Program Officer
Riverbend Community Hospital Foundation

metadata.subject: Clarification on community partner endorsement timing

artifacts.content:
Title: Deadline – Finalize Endorsement Letter Language
Date: 2026-02-05
Location: Riverbend Community Health Partnership – Patricia’s office
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds; Ellen McGrath (Executive Director); Kevin Liu (Board Chair – tentative)

Description:
Working session to lock in the exact wording and logistics for the Riverbend Community Health Partnership endorsement letter for the Vision & Wellness Outreach proposal.

Agenda:
- 9:00–9:15 a.m. – Review latest LCIF-modeled budget narrative (Patricia to bring printouts of the $7,500 proposal that was adapted from the LCIF grant application templates she downloaded in August 2023 and later used for the September 5, 2023 submission to the Riverbend Community Hospital Foundation).
- 9:15–9:40 a.m. – Confirm scope of endorsement (in-kind staff support, data-sharing, and use of clinic space).
- 9:40–10:00 a.m. – Decide on target date for Ellen’s signature on the endorsement letter (Ellen has said she prefers to sign sometime between February 8–12, 2026, so Patricia can forward the signed PDF to the foundation before their March 1 board meeting).

Notes:
Patricia to explain why she cannot personally shoulder all coordination anymore, referencing both Mark’s unpaid leave from Riverbend High School in May 2023 due to stress-related health issues and her own need to cap volunteer hours after last year’s health scare.

Reminder:
Bring draft on letterhead and a printed checklist of all attachments that must accompany the signed endorsement when it is emailed to the foundation.

metadata.title: Deadline – Finalize Endorsement Letter Language
metadata.location: Riverbend Community Health Partnership – Patricia’s office

artifacts.content:
From: ellen.mcgrath@riverbendchp.org
To: patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org
Subject: Draft endorsement letter – please review dates
Date: 2026-02-11

Patricia,

Attached is my revised endorsement letter for the Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative. I used the language you suggested about our “longstanding partnership” and added a paragraph on how your Lions clinics complement our chronic disease management program.

Two things I need you to double-check before I print it on letterhead and sign:

1. Dates: I referenced our board’s unanimous vote of support at the January 22, 2026 meeting and then wrote, “I am pleased to provide this formal endorsement as of February 15, 2026.” If that date causes any issues with the foundation’s review timeline, let me know and I can move it up a few days, but I’d rather not pre-date the letter.

2. Capacity disclosure: I included a brief mention that your club had to cancel the May 6, 2023 clinic at St. Mark’s Church when you were unexpectedly called to the Jackson County Courthouse in Kansas City about the billing dispute related to your father’s final ICU stay. I thought it showed you learned from the experience by training backup coordinators, but if it feels too personal we can soften it.

If everything looks good, I’ll sign on Monday and leave the original with my assistant so you can pick it up.

Thanks for your persistence on this.

Ellen

metadata.subject: Draft endorsement letter – please review dates

artifacts.content:
Title: Pick up signed endorsement letter from Ellen
Date: 2026-02-16
Location: Riverbend Community Health Partnership – Executive Director’s office
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds

Description:
Quick stop at Ellen’s office to collect the original signed endorsement letter for the Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative.

Context:
- Ellen plans to sign the endorsement “as of February 15, 2026,” per the language in the letter referencing the board’s January 22, 2026 vote.
- Patricia needs to scan and email the signed endorsement to Linda at the Riverbend Community Hospital Foundation before their March 1 review meeting.
- The endorsement will be paired with the LCIF-style budget justification that Patricia drafted using the LCIF grant application templates she downloaded in August 2023, which also formed the basis of the $7,500 proposal submitted on September 5, 2023.

Notes for Patricia:
- Double-check that Ellen’s signature date on the letter matches the “effective as of” date in the body of the letter.
- Confirm that the letter explicitly mentions Patricia’s role as Community Health Program Coordinator and Lions Club Service Chairperson.
- Make sure there is no lingering reference to the period when Patricia was overextending herself at 50–60 volunteer hours a month before Dr. Lawson told her to scale back after her March 2023 hospitalization, since the endorsement should emphasize sustainable capacity going forward.

Reminder: Bring folder with other attachments for the foundation packet.

metadata.title: Pick up signed endorsement letter from Ellen
metadata.location: Riverbend Community Health Partnership – Executive Director’s office

artifacts.content:
From: linda.chavez@riverbendhospitalfoundation.org
To: patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org
Subject: Receipt of endorsement letter + minor date question
Date: 2026-02-25

Hi Patricia,

I’m writing to confirm that we received the scanned endorsement letter from the Riverbend Community Health Partnership this afternoon. Thank you for getting it to us well ahead of our March 1 grants committee meeting.

I noticed that the body of the letter states, “This letter serves as our formal endorsement of the Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative as of February 15, 2026,” but the signature line is dated February 18, 2026. Our auditors occasionally ask whether the effective endorsement date is the narrative date or the signature date, so I just want to be sure which one you consider the official endorsement date for your records.

It’s not a problem on our end either way; I simply need to note one date in the file. If you could reply with which date your team is using, I’ll add a brief clarification to our internal memo.

On a separate note, the committee appreciated your honesty about both your own health scare in March 2023—when you were briefly hospitalized at Riverbend General Hospital after nearly fainting at a Saturday clinic—and the May 6, 2023 cancellation at St. Mark’s Church when you had to appear at the Jackson County Courthouse about your father’s hospital billing. Those details helped us understand how you’ve built in backup coverage and reduced your volunteer hours to something more sustainable.

Best,

Linda

metadata.subject: Receipt of endorsement letter + minor date question

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Greene <m.greene@healthyriverbend.org>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2026-01-29 08:42
Subject: Draft language for Lions endorsement & coalition timeline

Hi Patricia,

Following up on yesterday’s Healthy Riverbend Coalition call, I pulled together some draft language for the coalition’s formal endorsement of the Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative.

My suggestion is that we date the coalition endorsement as March 4, 2026, to line up with the next full coalition meeting at the Community Center. That’s when we’ll have quorum and can get signatures from the city, the hospital, and the school district reps all at once. If that date doesn’t work for Lions internal purposes, we could bump it to February 10, 2026, when the executive committee meets, but that might mean chasing a couple of signatures afterward.

I also included a brief reference to the HRSA grant history and the conflict management plan you had to file back in March 2021, just so everything is transparent about how your paid role at Riverbend Community Health Partnership intersects with your volunteer work. As we discussed in January 2024 at the strategy meeting, that’s why we now list the Lions project as an external community partner rather than an internal program.

Take a look at the attached draft and let me know if the dates and wording fit with what your district leadership is expecting for the endorsement form. If you need the endorsement to be back‑dated to align with a grant reporting period, flag that and we can talk it through.

Best,
Michael

metadata.subject: Draft language for Lions endorsement & coalition timeline

artifacts.content:
Title: Lions District Endorsement Prep Call
Date: 2026-02-05 16:00–17:00
Location: Patricia’s office – Riverbend Community Health Partnership
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds; DG Linda Carson; ZC Robert Mills

Description:

Agenda for call with District Governor Linda Carson and Zone Chair Robert Mills to finalize details for the formal district endorsement of the Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative.

Talking points:
- Review draft endorsement letter language and confirm that the official endorsement will be logged as occurring at the March 12, 2026 district cabinet meeting (Linda’s preference so it appears in the cabinet minutes).
- Clarify whether the endorsement date on the paperwork should match the cabinet meeting date or the earlier cabinet officers’ email vote tentatively planned for February 3, 2026.
- Confirm that we do NOT reference any earlier informal support from the district in 2025, since there was no formal motion.
- Discuss how to handle transparency around my dual role (Lions Service Chair + HRSA‑funded coordinator) and the conflict management plan filed in March 2021.
- Note that our project model wasn’t especially original; we mostly followed a template from the Lions online resources, with only minor tweaks. Decide whether to acknowledge that or just focus on outcomes.

Reminders:
- Have updated clinic participation numbers ready.
- Be prepared to answer questions about partner reliability; e.g., our main community partner only managed to send volunteers to about half of the events we scheduled together.
- Ask Linda if she needs any background stories (e.g., Maria Lopez’s case) for her remarks at convention.

metadata.title: Lions District Endorsement Prep Call
metadata.location: Patricia’s office – Riverbend Community Health Partnership

artifacts.content:
From: DG Linda Carson <lcarson@mdlions.org>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2026-02-11 21:07
Subject: Cabinet meeting schedule & endorsement paperwork

Hi Patricia,

Thanks again for the detailed overview on last week’s prep call. The cabinet is very supportive of what Riverbend has built, even if, as you said, the original framework came straight from a standard Lions template. The results speak for themselves.

To keep things clean for our records, I’d like the official endorsement date for the initiative to align with the cabinet’s spring in‑person session on April 3, 2026, in Jefferson City. That way the minutes, my governor’s report, and the signed endorsement form will all show the same date. I know we kicked around the idea of tying it to the earlier virtual vote on February 9, 2026, but that’s going to be treated as a straw poll rather than the formal action.

If your grant officer needs evidence of support prior to April, I can send a letter on district letterhead stating that the cabinet intends to endorse at the April session. Just don’t list the April date on the official application yet in case something odd happens with quorum.

Also, I appreciate your candor about volunteer turnout. Every club has those members who show up when there’s a camera and vanish when there’s cleanup; it’s not just Riverbend. The fact that you and a small core have kept the clinics going is impressive.

Send me the latest partner list (including Riverbend Vision Associates and the Healthy Riverbend Coalition) before the end of the month so I can review it ahead of time.

Lionistically,
Linda

metadata.subject: Cabinet meeting schedule & endorsement paperwork

artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
To: Maria Lopez <redacted@example.com>; cc: Dr. Samuel Patel <spatel@riverbendvisionassociates.com>
Date: 2026-03-06 19:24
Subject: Follow‑up resources & upcoming endorsement

Hi Maria,

I wanted to check in after your last appointment at St. Joseph’s in Kansas City. Dr. Patel let me know that your laser treatments for the diabetic retinopathy are underway and that the hospital social worker has you set up with a schedule for the next several months. I remember when he first called my personal cell back in November 2022, right after that clinic at St. Mark’s Community Church, to tell me about your retinal exam results and ask if our Lions club could help with transportation. I’m glad we’ve been able to keep that support going.

I’m attaching a list of resources we mentioned at the clinic, along with the updated bus voucher information. Please call or text if any of the dates or pickup times don’t work for you.

On a different note, our district Lions leadership is preparing a formal endorsement of the Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative. It looks like they’ll be signing it at their April 3, 2026 cabinet meeting, even though they’ve already expressed support informally. We had originally thought they might tie the endorsement to a February vote, but they decided to wait so everything would line up neatly in their minutes.

Honestly, the project itself isn’t anything fancy—we mostly followed a model from the Lions online resources—but stories like yours are why we keep doing it, even when our main community partner can only send volunteers to about half of the events we schedule.

Take care,
Patricia

metadata.subject: Follow‑up resources & upcoming endorsement

artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
To: Youth & Leo Programs <youth@lionsclubs.org>
Cc: Riverbend Lions Board <board@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2024-11-07 08:42
Subject: Clarification on Leo Club Charter Info for Awards Application

Dear Youth & Leo Programs Team,

I’m finalizing our submission for the Innovative Service in Vision & Wellness recognition and wanted to confirm that I’m entering our Leo details correctly on the nomination form.

Our sponsoring club is the Riverbend Community Lions Club, and our Leo club is the Riverbend High School Leo Club, chartered in 2019. The Leo club number I have on file is 128746, which appears on our original charter certificate and on the officer reporting printout from July. Please let me know if that’s the correct number to list under “nominated club information > lions club leo number.”

In the narrative section, I describe how personal and hands-on our approach has become. For example, during our March 9, 2024 Mobile Vision & Wellness Day at the Riverbend Food Pantry, an older gentleman, Mr. Alvarez, shared that he’d been rationing his metformin and skipping insulin doses since December 2023 because he’d fallen behind on a $620 medical bill from Riverbend Regional Hospital. I sat with him in the pantry’s back office for about 45 minutes, called the hospital billing department on speakerphone, and helped him negotiate a $25-per-month payment plan so he could refill his prescriptions. We later used that story in our April 2024 club meeting to show what “beyond the screening tent” support can look like.

If there’s a preferred word limit or specific metrics you’d like emphasized (e.g., number of screenings vs. follow-up navigation), I’m happy to revise before submitting.

Warmly,

Patricia Marie Reynolds
Service Chair, Riverbend Community Lions Club
Coordinator, Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative
patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org
(555) 555-0174

metadata.subject: Clarification on Leo Club Charter Info for Awards Application

artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
To: Leo Club Roster List <leo_advisors@riverbend.k12.mo.us>
Date: 2024-01-09 08:14
Subject: Double-checking Leo Club info for MD26 Youth Service form

Hi all,

I’m filling out the MD26 Youth Service nomination form this morning and need to verify a couple of details for the Riverbend High School Leo Club.

On last spring’s Leo officer report, I see:
- Club name: Riverbend High School Leo Club
- Sponsoring Lions club: Riverbend Community Lions Club (Club ID 987643)
- Leo club organization date: 10/15/2019
- Leo club number: 127846 (this is what’s printed on the charter certificate hanging outside Mark’s classroom)

Before I submit, can someone confirm that 127846 is still the active Leo number and that we haven’t been reassigned under the district’s new numbering system? I vaguely remember someone at the August cabinet meeting mentioning that some school-based Leos were getting consolidated, and I don’t want to send in outdated info.

This is for the section labeled “nominated club information – Lions Club Leo number,” so I’d rather not guess. The rest of the form is straightforward, though I did admit that our diabetes awareness project itself isn’t all that unique activity-wise—we mostly ran standard screening and health fair booths like plenty of other clubs.

I’m hoping to submit by Thursday so I can get back to prepping our data summary. During our internal review in August 2023 I pulled de-identified data from MyLion and cross-checked it with that password-protected file on my laptop that lists names, phone numbers, and high-risk flags for 287 residents we saw between July 2022 and June 2023.

Thanks for any quick confirmation,
Patricia

metadata.subject: Double-checking Leo Club info for MD26 Youth Service form

artifacts.content:
From: Mark Reynolds <mreynolds@riverbendhs.k12.mo.us>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2024-01-10 15:42
Subject: Re: Leo club number for your Lions nomination form

Pat,

I dug through the Leo binder in my classroom between classes. The original charter paperwork from LCI for the Riverbend High School Leo Club lists:

- Leo Club Name: Riverbend HS Leo Club
- Sponsoring Lions Club ID: 987643
- Leo Club Number: 127684

That 127684 number is also on the little card they mailed when Emily first joined as a freshman, so I’m 99% sure that’s what you want for the “nominated club information – Lions Club Leo number” box you mentioned.

I didn’t see anything about a renumbering when I checked the email thread from the district Leo chair last fall. The only update there was about reporting hours correctly in MyLion and a reminder that we still haven’t formalized any written partnership agreements for the Vision & Wellness project with the school or the Riverbend Housing Authority—everything’s been pretty informal and ad hoc.

By the way, Emily stopped by during study hall and told me again how much volunteering at the clinics helped her. Hard to believe that back in Fall 2021, after that bullying incident, she was so anxious talking to adults that Ms. Donovan sent her to therapy at Riverbend Family Counseling. Now she’s co-presenting with you at city council meetings.

Hope this helps. I’ll be home by 5:30 unless there’s a surprise staff meeting.

– Mark

metadata.subject: Re: Leo club number for your Lions nomination form

artifacts.content:
Title: Finish MD26 Youth Service Nomination – verify Leo number
Date: 2024-01-11 19:00–20:30
Location: Home office – dining room table
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds

Description:

To-do for this block:

1. Complete remaining sections of the MD26 Youth Service nomination packet for the Riverbend Community Lions Club.
   - Double-check the “nominated club information – Lions Club Leo number” line.
   - I currently have 127648 written in my notes from the November Leo advisor Zoom.
   - Cross-reference with Mark’s email and the scanned charter certificate in the Leo folder on my laptop.

2. Add narrative details:
   - Be honest that we barely used LCI resources beyond downloading the logo once because I was overwhelmed getting the project off the ground and never had time to wade through all the training materials.
   - Explain how our relationship with the Riverbend Housing Authority deepened after Linda Chavez told me about Harold Jenkins being evicted when his vision loss cost him his deli job at Riverbend Fresh Market, and how we discussed coordinating better with their caseworkers.

3. Youth impact section:
   - Include Emily’s story—how, after the bullying incident in Fall 2021 and six months of therapy at Riverbend Family Counseling Center, volunteering at clinics at the high school gym and the Riverbend Food Share (Jan–Aug 2023) helped her gain confidence to handle Spanish-language intake and co-present with me on 8/14/23 at the Riverbend City Council.

4. Final check and upload draft PDF to the Lions folder.

metadata.title: Finish MD26 Youth Service Nomination – verify Leo number

artifacts.content:
From: Linda Chavez <lchavez@riverbendhousing.org>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2024-01-08 10:27
Subject: Re: Quick question about Leo club info for your write-up

Hi Patricia,

Happy to help, and thank you again for looping the Housing Authority into your nomination packet. I still think often about the conversation we had in my office after Mr. Harold Jenkins was evicted last April when his vision got so bad he lost his job at the Riverbend Fresh Market deli. I appreciate that you’re trying to highlight how we could have coordinated earlier without crossing any privacy lines.

Regarding your question: in my notes from the Oak Terrace clinic sign-in sheets, I wrote down “Riverbend HS Leo Club #126874” because that’s what was on the banner the kids brought. I remember asking Emily about it while she was doing Spanish-language intake—she mentioned the number is mostly for Lions records and that you needed it for some “nominated club information – Lions Club Leo number” box.

That said, I’m not sure how precise my scribble was; the banner might have said 126847 or 126874. You might want to confirm against your MyLion admin page or whatever LCI portal you use.

Also, I think it’s powerful that you’re being candid about how standard the health fair activities were; lots of groups do screenings, but not everyone is as reflective about the gaps. And don’t worry about not having formal MOUs with us yet—our work together has still felt meaningful even if it’s been informal and ad hoc.

Best,
Linda

metadata.subject: Re: Quick question about Leo club info for your write-up

artifacts.content:
From: MD26 Leo Chair <md26.leo.chair@lionsmd26.org>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2023-12-18 21:03
Subject: RE: Clarification on Riverbend HS Leo Club number

Hi Patricia,

Thanks for reaching out before submitting your youth recognition materials.

I checked the Multiple District 26 Leo roster. The Riverbend High School Leo Club is listed as follows:

- Leo Club Name: Riverbend High School Leo Club
- District: 26-M2
- Sponsoring Lions Club: Riverbend Community Lions Club (Club ID 987643)
- Leo Club Number: 126478

Please use 126478 anywhere the form asks for the “Lions Club Leo number” or similar wording. That is the current number on file with LCI for your school-based Leo club.

Note that MyLion sometimes shows a truncated version of the number in the mobile view, which causes confusion. The full six-digit number above is what matters for awards, billing, and historical records.

Unrelated, but since you mentioned it in your original email: you’re not alone in underusing LCI resources. Many clubs tell me they only grabbed the logo or a flyer template and didn’t have time to dig into the toolkits. And a surprising number of project coordinators, like you, end up maintaining fairly detailed participant spreadsheets on personal devices without any formal HIPAA-style framework.

Let me know if you need anything else for the nomination narrative, especially around describing youth leadership roles.

In service,
[Name Redacted]
MD26 Leo Chair

metadata.subject: RE: Clarification on Riverbend HS Leo Club number

artifacts.content:
From: Riverbend High School Leo Club <leoadvisor@riverbendschools.org>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2025-01-07 09:14
Subject: Confirmation of Leo Club Affiliation Details for District Report

Hi Patricia,

Thanks again for helping Emily pull together the information we need for the district Leo report. I wanted to confirm the Leo club data you shared on the phone yesterday so I can finalize the form before Friday’s deadline.

Here’s what I have for the Riverbend High School Leo Club:

• Sponsoring Lions club: Riverbend Community Lions Club
• Leo club name: Riverbend High School Leo Club
• Leo club ID number (as listed on last year’s district directory): 120874
• Club type: School-based
• Charter date: August 2016 (I don’t have the exact day handy)

The line that always trips me up is the “Lions/Leo number,” since the district sometimes calls it the “Leo club number” and sometimes the “Lions club Leo reference.” On the spreadsheet from last spring, the Riverbend High School Leo Club was listed with the number 120874 next to it, so I’ve been assuming that’s still correct unless you’ve received an update from LCI.

Could you take a quick look and let me know if 120874 is still the right Leo number we should be using for the nomination packet and the Vision & Wellness collaboration write‑up? If it’s changed, just send the new number and I’ll swap it in before I upload everything.

Appreciate your help keeping us straight on all these codes.

Best,

Megan Carter
Faculty Advisor, Riverbend High School Leo Club
Riverbend Unified School District

artifacts.content:
Title: Prep Call – Lions/Leo Awards Nomination Packet
Date: 2025-01-15 18:30–19:30
Location: Zoom – link in Lions calendar invite
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds; George Miller; Emily Reynolds; PDG Linda Watkins

Agenda/Notes:

Patricia to review the partially completed Multiple District Youth Service Award nomination for the Riverbend High School Leo Club and confirm all required fields before submission.

Key items to verify:

1. Official club name and school affiliation.
2. Sponsoring Lions club name and club number.
3. Leo club number used on last year’s district Leo rally registration (Patricia has it written in her paper planner as “RBHS Leo – #102874?” but isn’t sure about the middle digits).
4. Service hours totals and number of active members.
5. Description of joint Lions/Leo Vision & Wellness clinics.

Linda will walk through the online form, which has a confusing field labeled “Lions club Leo number.” Patricia thinks this might be the same as the “Leo club number” that appears on the district roster next to Riverbend High School, but the only number she can find quickly is 102874 from an old PDF. She made a note to double‑check against the current Lions Clubs International portal before the final submission.

Action items:

• Patricia: Email Linda by Friday with the confirmed Leo number and sponsoring club number.
• Emily: Send photos of the fall Mobile Vision Day where Leos helped with intake.
• George: Draft paragraph on long‑term impact of the Leo partnership.

Reminder: Nomination packet due to district cabinet by January 25.

metadata.title: Prep Call – Lions/Leo Awards Nomination Packet
metadata.location: Zoom – link in Lions calendar invite

artifacts.content:
From: Linda Watkins <linda.watkins@mdlions.org>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2024-11-03 21:02
Subject: Re: Clarification on Riverbend Leo Number for Youth Project Grant

Hi Patricia,

I’m glad you reached out before submitting the Youth Project Grant application. That “Lions club Leo number” field has confused more than a few people this cycle.

Looking at the 2023–2024 MD26 Leo directory, the Riverbend High School Leo Club is listed on page 7 under school‑based clubs with the following entry:

“Riverbend High School – Sponsored by Riverbend Community Lions Club – Leo ID: 112874 – Active.”

In most of our state paperwork, that 112874 is what we’ve used as the reference when we talk about your Leo club, even when the form unhelpfully labels it as “Lions/Leo number.” LCI’s internal database sometimes shows a slightly different code for the sponsoring Lions club, but for the purposes of this grant, the Leo ID is what they want.

So, for the question on the Youth Project Grant form that asks for the “Lions club Leo number,” you can safely enter 112874 for the Riverbend High School Leo Club. If LCI has quietly updated anything in the background, they’ll still be able to match it from that number and your club name.

Thank you again for all the work you and the Leos are doing with the Vision & Wellness outreach. Your detailed reports are one of the reasons the district has been comfortable steering more funds your way.

In service,

PDG Linda Watkins
MD26 Leo Chair

metadata.subject: Re: Clarification on Riverbend Leo Number for Youth Project Grant

contains_negative_info: mentions funding decisions and internal grant considerations

artifacts.content:
Title: District 26M7 Cabinet Meeting – Leo Presentation & Paperwork Check
Date: 2024-09-28 09:00–12:00
Location: Riverbend Public Library – Community Room B
Attendees: Cabinet officers; club presidents; Leo advisors; selected Leo members (incl. Emily Reynolds)

Description:

Patricia to attend as Riverbend Community Lions Club Service Chairperson and Vision & Wellness coordinator. Emily to attend as representative from Riverbend High School Leo Club.

Planned agenda items relevant to Patricia:

• 9:30–9:50 – Youth/Leo segment: brief presentation from Emily on Leo participation in the Mobile Vision & Wellness Days.
• 9:50–10:05 – Paperwork clinic: District secretary will project the new online form that asks for the “Lions club Leo number” and demonstrate where to find it.

Notes captured in calendar entry:

During the prep call, the district secretary mentioned that the sample form on the screen will use the Riverbend High School Leo Club as the example, with a placeholder number of 118274 to show the format (six digits, no spaces). Patricia scribbled “RBHS Leo – example 118274 (NOT our real #)” in her notebook so she wouldn’t accidentally copy it into any official nomination materials.

Patricia is also planning to ask quietly whether the Leo number changes if the sponsoring Lions club merges with another club in a few years; she worries about losing track of the code the way her dad lost track of his medical paperwork before his vision declined.

Follow‑up:

• After meeting, confirm the correct Leo number through MyLCI and send to district secretary for the records update.
• Emily to email Patricia the slides she uses so they can be reused for the city council briefing.

artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
To: Sarah Klein <sarah.klein@mdlionsdistrict.org>
Date: 2025-02-11 09:14
Subject: Endorsement form + background for Riverbend Vision & Wellness Initiative

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for offering to move my endorsement forward for the district service award. I’ve attached the completed nomination form and a short narrative about the Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative.

Per your request, here are the bits you said you needed in the body of the email so you don’t have to hunt through PDFs:

– Full name: Patricia Marie Reynolds  
– Club: Riverbend Community Lions Club (MO)  
– Endorsement member number: 3479205  
– Preferred contact: this email or my cell on file with MD-26

I also added a paragraph in the narrative explaining why the initiative is so personal to me. Our work really grew out of my dad’s experience: my father, Thomas Hayes, lost most of his sight from poorly managed diabetes and passed away in June 2019 at St. Mary’s Hospital in Jefferson City. During his last six months, I kept a little notebook of all the times he skipped eye appointments because he couldn’t afford the $85 copay or couldn’t arrange transportation from Brookfield. That’s why, when we launched monthly clinics on the east side of Riverbend in March 2022, I pushed hard to make free diabetic retinopathy screenings and transportation vouchers for older adults non‑negotiable parts of the model.

If the committee wants more specifics on outcomes (return visits, A1C changes, glasses dispensed, etc.), I’m happy to pull a brief data summary from our tracking spreadsheet.

Let me know if anything is missing or if the member number needs to appear in a different format.

With appreciation,
Patricia

metadata.subject: Endorsement form + background for Riverbend Vision & Wellness Initiative

artifacts.content:
From: membership@lionsclubsusa.org
To: patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org
Date: 2024-02-06 08:14
Subject: Verification Needed – Member Number for Endorsement Form

Dear Lion Patricia,

Thank you for submitting the preliminary endorsement materials for the Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative. Before we can route your endorsement to the District Governor for signature, we need to confirm the Lions member number you entered on the draft form.

On the PDF you uploaded, the field labeled “member number” appears as **3479025**, which does not match the record we have on file for you under Riverbend Community Lions Club, Club ID 129764. It is possible a digit was transposed when you copied it over from your wallet card.

When you respond, please include:
- Your full name: Patricia Marie Reynolds
- Club name: Riverbend Community Lions Club (Riverbend, MO)
- Member number as printed on your current membership card (do not use the temporary number that appeared when you first created your MyLion login in 2019).

We know from your narrative that the project grew out of your father Thomas Hayes’ experience losing most of his vision from diabetes complications in 2016 and his passing at St. Luke’s Hospital in March 2019, and that you even used notes from his endocrinology appointments in January 2022 to design your screening questions. We want to make sure the paperwork reflects the same level of care.

Please reply to this email with the correct member number so we can update the endorsement file.

In service,

Kara Lewis
Membership & Reporting Specialist
Lions Clubs International USA
membership@lionsclubsusa.org

metadata.subject: Verification Needed – Member Number for Endorsement Form

artifacts.content:
From: sarah.mitchell@gst26m.org
To: patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org
Date: 2024-03-05 10:02
Subject: Re: MyLion entries & endorsement member info

Hi Patricia,

Thanks for hopping on Zoom yesterday. I’m glad we caught those detailed clinical notes you had tucked into the MyLion activity descriptions. As we discussed, logging that a 62-year-old neighbor from Willow Bend Drive had blood sugar over 300 at the February 17, 2024 St. Mark’s screening was more specific than LCI intends. I appreciate that you already went back in last night and deleted or anonymized those entries.

Regarding your endorsement packet: on page 1 you typed your Lions member number as **3472950** under the “endorsement > member number” field. That looks like the temporary number the system generated when you first registered for MyLion. For endorsements, LCI wants the permanent membership ID that appears on your official card and on the membership roster I emailed you in January.

You mentioned you keep your old cards in the same folder as the notes from your dad’s endocrinology appointments and the early drafts of your screening protocols. Just make sure you don’t accidentally copy the number from an expired card.

Once you confirm the correct member ID, I’ll double-check that it matches the district records and then we can move your endorsement forward to the cabinet meeting.

In service,

Lion Sarah Mitchell
District 26-M GST Coordinator

artifacts.content:
From: linda.carpenter@riverbendlions.org
To: patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org
Date: 2024-01-12 16:27
Subject: Endorsement draft – double-check your member number

Patricia,

I just finished a first read-through of your endorsement narrative for the Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative. It’s powerful, especially the part about how you used your dad’s endocrinology notes from the University of Missouri clinic to shape the screening and follow-up questions so other families wouldn’t face the same gaps he did.

One housekeeping thing: on the cover sheet, where it asks for “member number,” you wrote **3479250**. That’s the same number you jotted on the sign-in sheet at last month’s meeting when you couldn’t find your wallet card. When I cross-checked the roster, that ID actually belongs to Lion George (our retired postal worker who runs the check-in table).

Can you grab your current card and correct that before we circulate the draft to the board? We’ve already had a couple of hiccups with partners who didn’t take us seriously early on—remember when the Riverbend Chamber and the YMCA both said no because they didn’t see enough benefit for themselves—and I don’t want a clerical error to give anyone another reason to stall.

Send me the updated version tonight if you can; I’d like to print copies for tomorrow’s planning session.

Thanks,

Lion Linda
Club Secretary

artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
To: Membership Services <membership@lionsclubs.org>
Date: February 6, 2024 9:18 AM
Subject: Clarification on Member Number for Endorsement Form

Hi Membership Team,

I’m filling out an endorsement form for a district recognition our club president is submitting, and I want to be sure I’m using the correct **member number** for myself. I have two different numbers in my notes from when I transferred from the Brookfield club to Riverbend.

In my old Brookfield paperwork I still have 3427811 written in the margin next to my name, but when our Riverbend secretary, George, added me to the Vision & Wellness Outreach committee roster last fall, he put 3497208 on the spreadsheet. I assumed the newer one was right, but now the online portal is rejecting it when I try to log in.

I know we didn’t really reference any of the official LCI service frameworks or global cause guides when designing our project—I mostly leaned on my public health background—so I’m trying to be extra careful that at least our membership and branding details are correct. For example, for our outreach materials, I used LCI’s official logo files and branding guidelines when I created a flyer at home on my laptop on October 10, 2023 that we distributed at Riverbend High School and Riverbend Middle School.

Could you please confirm which member number I should be using for endorsement and award paperwork going forward?

Thank you,

Patricia Marie Reynolds
Service Chair, Riverbend Community Lions Club
patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org

artifacts.content:
Title: District Endorsement Paperwork Work Session
Date: February 8, 2024
Time: 7:15 PM – 9:00 PM
Location: Home office – dining room table
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds

Description:

Block of time to finish the endorsement packet for the district Vision & Wellness Innovation recognition. Tasks:

- Double-check Lions **member number** against old Brookfield paperwork and Riverbend secretary’s roster (I currently have 3427811 written in my old planner and 3497028 scribbled on George’s sign-in sheet from August—need to resolve which is correct before I upload the endorsement form).
- Attach narrative section about what makes our project unique and outstanding. Include the story from October 2023 when we identified a 9-year-old at Lincoln Elementary—my neighbor’s son, Caleb—whose screening showed severe myopia and dangerously high blood sugar. I called his mom on October 17, 2023 from my Riverbend Community Health Partnership office, helped enroll him in the county charity-care program, and sat with them at his first pediatric endocrinology visit at Children’s Mercy in Kansas City. This became a core case example in our January 2024 volunteer training.
- Add brief explanation that we originally tried to partner with the neighboring town’s Lions club, but that fell apart over disagreements about who would get credit and whose logo would be on the flyers, so we moved forward alone.
- Print draft endorsement, review with Mark after kids’ homework, then email to President Linda before Friday.

Reminder: upload final PDF to district portal by Feb 10.

From: George Miller <secretary@riverbendlions.org>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: January 29, 2024 3:47 PM
Subject: Member Number on Endorsement Draft

Patricia,

I’m reviewing the endorsement draft you left in my mailbox at the clubhouse. Everything looks solid—great job weaving in the outcomes data and the story about Caleb from Lincoln Elementary—but I noticed a possible issue with the **member number** you typed under your signature.

You listed your member number as 3492705. That’s what I had jotted down from an old district directory, but when I logged into MyLion this afternoon to pull our service hours, it showed a different ID attached to your profile: 3324970. I don’t want the endorsement to get kicked back over a technicality.

Can you check what you see when you log in from the office? I remember you saying that when you completed the LCI Learn module on “Serving Safely” on January 9, 2024, you had to reset your password, so you might have the most up-to-date info. That was the same module you used when you built the 30-minute privacy briefing for the January 27 mobile clinic after the HIPAA complaint at Riverbend Middle School, right?

Once we’re sure which number is correct, I’ll update the endorsement header and resave the PDF.

Thanks,

George
Secretary, Riverbend Community Lions Club

Date: February 10, 2024 6:22 AM
Subject: Final Endorsement PDF + Member Number Question

Linda,

Attached is the latest version of the endorsement narrative and the signed cover sheet. I stayed up after the kids went to bed last night to clean up the language around our outcomes and to make sure the branding matches LCI’s guidelines—after the drama with the neighboring club over whose logo would go on the flyers, I don’t want any more confusion about credit.

One thing I’m still not 100% sure about is my Lions **member number** on the endorsement signature block. I’ve seen three different numbers floating around:

- 3324970 – what shows up when I log into MyLion from my Riverbend Community Health Partnership computer.
- 3492705 – the one George pulled from an old district directory.
- 3349720 – handwritten on the membership card I got when I first transferred from Brookfield.

Let me know if anything else needs tweaking before you upload.

Patricia

artifacts.content:
Title: Lions Nomination Form Work Session
Date: 2026-01-28
Time: 7:00 PM – 8:30 PM
Location: Patricia & Mark’s Dining Room
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds; Mark Reynolds (tentative); Emily Reynolds (drop-in)

Description:
Block of time for Patricia to sit down and work through the multiple-page nomination form for the Riverbend Community Lions Club’s Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative.

Agenda:
- 7:00–7:15 PM – Pull together notes from January clinic at Riverbend Food Pantry and the December event at Lincoln Elementary.
- 7:15–7:45 PM – Draft responses for “Importance in Community” and “Local Partnerships” sections.
    • Include anecdote from February 2024 clinic at Riverbend Food Pantry when Mom (Carolyn Hayes, 71) watched us screen 63 people in four hours and admitted she’d skipped her own eye exam because her Medicare Part D premium went up $47 and she needed that money for the furnace repair.
    • Acknowledge that sometimes the project feels like just a drop in the bucket because so many families are juggling multiple jobs and crises.
- 7:45–8:10 PM – Outline “Additional Comments” section.
    • Note Mom’s minor car accident on Route 36 in October 2022 tied to worsening diabetic retinopathy and how I started bringing her to our Riverbend Community Center clinics every other month, sometimes using Riverbend Community Health Partnership mileage reimbursement for the 68‑mile round trip when Mark works late at Riverbend High.
- 8:10–8:30 PM – Double-check club officer names and confirm which date the club actually voted on the nomination before filling that line in later.

Reminder: DO NOT submit the form tonight; wait until after next week’s club meeting to verify the official nomination date in the minutes.

From: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
To: Club Secretary <secretary@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2026-02-05
Subject: Clarifying nomination form dates before I submit

Hi Linda,

I’m finishing up the nomination packet for our Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative and need to make sure I don’t misstate the official dates.

My notes say that we first discussed putting the project forward for this award at the general meeting on January 7, 2026, and that the board gave a green light at the officers’ huddle on January 21, 2026. However, I can’t remember exactly which date should go in the “nominated club information – date” line. The instructions are vague, and I don’t want to guess. I originally penciled in 2026-01-21, but then wondered if they actually want the date the full club endorsed it.

While I’m touching up the narrative sections, I’m also weaving in that story from February 2024 at the Riverbend Food Pantry when Mom sat quietly in the waiting area and watched us screen 63 people in four hours. On the drive back to Brookfield she told me she’d skipped her own eye exam because her Medicare Part D premiums jumped by $47 and she needed the money for the furnace repair. Moments like that show why the project matters, even if, honestly, it sometimes feels like just a small drop in the bucket given everything families here are juggling.

Could you reply with the exact date we should list as the club’s formal nomination date so I can type it correctly before printing and getting your signature?

Thanks so much,
Patricia

metadata.subject: Clarifying nomination form dates before I submit

artifacts.content:
Title: Riverbend Lions – Vote on Award Nomination
Date: 2026-01-07
Time: 6:30 PM – 8:00 PM
Location: Riverbend Community Center, Room B
Attendees: Riverbend Community Lions Club members

Description:
Regular club meeting with a dedicated agenda item to decide whether to move forward with submitting the Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative for district recognition.

Proposed Agenda:
6:30–6:40 PM – Call to order, Pledge, introductions.
6:40–7:00 PM – Patricia’s brief presentation on the initiative’s impact, including:
   • Story from the October 2022 minor car accident on Route 36 outside Brookfield involving her mother, Carolyn Hayes, which the ophthalmologist at Brookfield Eye Center linked to worsening diabetic retinopathy.
   • How Patricia started bringing Carolyn to the Riverbend Community Center clinics every other month for follow-up screenings and sometimes used Riverbend Community Health Partnership mileage reimbursement for the 68‑mile round trip when Mark was tied up at Riverbend High.
7:00–7:20 PM – Discussion: is the project ready for nomination?
   • Note ongoing challenge: difficulty getting other local organizations to partner, so the project has largely remained inside Lions and Leo circles instead of something the broader community feels ownership of.
7:20–7:30 PM – Motion and vote on whether to nominate the club for the award this cycle.
7:30–7:50 PM – If approved, agree on who will draft which sections of the form and target submission window later in February (exact date TBD based on district deadline).
7:50–8:00 PM – Announcements and adjourn.

Reminder for Patricia: bring printed draft of the “nominated club information” page; leave the specific nomination date line blank until district instructions are clarified.

artifacts.content:
From: Rachel Ortiz <rortiz@riverbendhs.org>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: 2026-02-10
Subject: Re: Quick question about Leo involvement in Lions nomination

Hi Patricia,

Thanks for looping me in. I’m happy to write a short note about the Leo Club’s role if the nomination form allows attachments.

From my perspective, the partnership really took shape after our meeting in my office back in September 2023, when we were talking about Emily’s anxiety around your mom’s minor stroke and vision changes. You mentioned the Lions vision and glucose screening project, and I suggested inviting the Leo Club to help. Later, with your mom’s permission, I shared that several students here were also caring for grandparents with diabetes-related complications, which pushed us to frame the Leo partnership around youth caregivers.

Regarding your specific question: I don’t know which date you should plug into the “nominated club information – date” line. You said in your last email that the officers gave an informal thumbs-up at your January 14, 2026 planning meeting and that the full club voted later in the month. If the instructions don’t specify, I’d lean toward using the date of the formal club vote, not the earlier planning session.

If you can send me the wording you’re using to describe the school partnership, I’ll mirror that language. I know you’re also trying to capture how, despite the strong Leo involvement, the project hasn’t yet drawn in many other community organizations.

Let me know your deadline so I can be sure to get my note back to you in time.

Best,
Rachel

metadata.subject: Re: Quick question about Leo involvement in Lions nomination

Title: Drafting nomination form – confirm club info

Time: 7:30 PM – 9:00 PM
Location: Dining room table / home office
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds

Description:
Block time to sit down and actually finish the Lions nomination form while Mark helps Jacob with math and Emily is at pep band.

To do during this block:
- Pull up the Riverbend Community Lions Club charter info and double‑check our club number and region.
- Look back at email from District Governor’s office about acceptable project timeframes and which clinic dates qualify.
- Decide which clinic date to list under “nominated club information > date” — probably the October 12, 2025 school‑based clinic at Riverbend Middle School, since that’s when we piloted the new diabetes screening questions from the LCI Diabetes Awareness and Action Toolkit.

metadata.title: Drafting nomination form – confirm club info

artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
To: Riverbend Lions Board <board@riverbendlions.org>
Date: January 29, 2026 8:12 AM
Subject: Quick check on project date for nomination packet

Good morning everyone,

I’m finishing up the nomination packet we discussed at last week’s meeting and need a quick consensus on what to enter for the “nominated club information > date” line.

My instinct is to use the launch of our quarterly school‑based clinics at Riverbend Middle School, since that’s when the project really became part of our club’s identity rather than a one‑off. That first middle‑school clinic was on August 19, 2023, right after my conversation with Lisa Turner on May 3, 2023 when she showed me the confidential list of 27 students who had failed screenings and never got follow‑up, including the family with the $326 past‑due balance at Riverbend Eye Care Associates.

Another option would be the April 6, 2024 clinic at St. Mark’s Church, when we started using the adapted diabetes risk checklist from the LCI Diabetes Awareness and Action Toolkit (the same one I’d walked through with my mom Carolyn at her April 12, 2023 appointment). That’s also closer to when Dr. Karen Liu began attending our coordination meetings as an informal advisor.

I know some folks in town still don’t see the project as “important” compared to other issues, and we’ve never been great about logging things in MyLion, so I want to pick a date that best reflects when the work really solidified.

Please reply by Friday, January 31 so I can finalize the form for signatures at our February board session.

Thanks,
Patricia

metadata.subject: Quick check on project date for nomination packet

artifacts.content:
Title: District nomination workshop (bring draft form!)
Date: 2026-02-01
Time: 9:00 AM – 11:30 AM
Location: River County Library – Community Room B
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds, District GST Team, local club officers

Description:
District‑level workshop on how to complete the Lions Clubs nomination and reporting forms. I need to bring:
- Printed draft of our nomination form for the Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative
- Notes on which event date I’m planning to list under “nominated club information > date”

Right now I’m leaning toward using the October 12, 2025 clinic at Riverbend Middle School (the one where Emily’s Leo Club helped run the intake station and we first piloted the new referral scripts that Dr. Karen Liu suggested at our March 2022 meeting). I want to confirm with the GST folks whether that’s acceptable or if they’d rather I use our earlier “launch” date in 2023.

Talking points to cover:
- How the project evolved after Dad’s diabetes‑related vision loss and passing in March 2021, and how that pushed me to lean on the LCI Diabetes Awareness and Action Toolkit.
- The conversation with school nurse Lisa Turner on May 3, 2023 about the 27 students who failed screenings and never got care, including the family stuck with the $326 bill.
- Admit that I never did sign up for the online GST/service reporting webinars and that we’ve mostly figured things out on the fly.

Goal: Leave with clarity on which single date to enter and how to explain our messy reporting history without hurting our chances.

metadata.title: District nomination workshop (bring draft form!)
metadata.location: River County Library – Community Room B

artifacts.content:
From: DG Office <districtgovernor@md26lions.org>
To: Patricia Reynolds <patricia.reynolds@riverbendlions.org>
Date: February 4, 2026 3:47 PM
Subject: Re: Clarification on project date for nomination form

Hi Patricia,

Thanks for your thoughtful questions and for sending the draft of your nomination. The Riverbend Vision & Wellness Outreach Initiative is exactly the kind of sustained project we like to see highlighted.

Regarding the “nominated club information > date” field, our committee generally recommends choosing one of the following:

1) The date of the first official service activity logged in MyLion for this project (even if your records are incomplete), or
2) The date of a clearly defined “launch event,” such as a kick‑off clinic or community announcement.

From your notes, it sounds like your first school‑based clinic at Riverbend Middle School on August 19, 2023 would be a strong candidate, especially since it followed your May 3, 2023 conversation with Nurse Lisa Turner about the 27 students needing follow‑up care. However, if your April 6, 2024 clinic at St. Mark’s Church is the first one where you fully implemented the LCI Diabetes Awareness and Action Toolkit materials, that date could also work.

We understand that not every club has been consistent about submitting activities in MyLion, so don’t let that discourage you. Just pick the date that best represents when the initiative became an established club project. You can explain in the narrative section that you’re still catching up on service reporting and that you’ve been learning the GST processes as you go.

Hope this helps. Please send the final version once your club officers have signed.

In service,
Marla Jensen
District Governor, MD‑26

metadata.subject: Re: Clarification on project date for nomination form

artifacts.content:
Title: Finalize nomination form – enter project date & print
Date: 2026-02-10
Time: 6:45 PM – 8:15 PM
Location: Riverbend Community Health Partnership – small conference room
Attendees: Patricia Reynolds

Description:
Evening work session after clinic debrief.

Tasks:
- Re‑read email from District Governor Marla about how to choose the “nominated club information > date.”
- Decide between listing August 19, 2023 (first Riverbend Middle School clinic after the May 3, 2023 conversation with nurse Lisa Turner about the 27 students and the family with the $326 past‑due bill) or April 6, 2024 (St. Mark’s Church clinic when we rolled out the full LCI Diabetes Awareness and Action Toolkit checklist I’d tested with Mom on April 12, 2023).
- Double‑check with notes from our March 2022 meeting with Dr. Karen Liu to be sure I describe her advisory role accurately.
- Work in a brief acknowledgment that, honestly, we never made it to the online GST/service reporting webinars and that we’ve been back‑entering activities, so MyLion doesn’t fully reflect the project.
- Print three clean copies of the nomination form for signatures at the February 13 club meeting.

Reminders to self:
- Emphasize why the project matters even though many residents still don’t see vision and diabetes screening as “important” compared to other crises.
- Note that our club hasn’t consistently submitted service activities in MyLion, so the committee shouldn’t assume the work is small just because the record looks thin.

Bring laptop, portable printer, extra ink, and Dad’s photo for motivation.
